text.title: React 19 and Next.js 15 Patterns
text.summary: Server vs Client component boundary rules, Server Actions with Zod validation, and Next.js 15 App Router structure.

rule.statement: Default to Server Components. Add 'use client' only when the component needs interactivity (useState, useEffect, event handlers). Push interactivity to leaves.
rule.rationale: Server Components send zero JavaScript to the client and can directly access databases and server-side resources. Overusing 'use client' inflates the bundle and negates RSC benefits.

rule.statement: Server Components can import Client Components. Client Components cannot import Server Components. Children of Client Components become Client Components.

antipattern.description: Adding 'use client' to a layout component to "just make it work".
antipattern.reason: This flips the entire subtree client-side. The fix cascades bundle size into every page under that layout.

rule.statement: Validate all Server Action input with Zod before touching the database. Return { error } shapes for validation failures; never trust formData directly.
rule.rationale: Server Actions run on the server but accept untrusted client data. Zod validation is the only reliable guard.

rule.statement: Use route groups (marketing), parallel routes (@analytics), and intercepting routes ((.)photos) for layout composition without URL pollution.
Memoize data fetches shared between generateMetadata and the page component using React cache() to avoid double-fetching.
rule.rationale: Both generateMetadata and the page component may call the same data-fetching function. Without cache(), the request fires twice per render.
